input:
His pretext was that his opinion was overlooked in favour of that of the more enthusiastic Muslims who wanted to engage the enemy straight away. He overlooked the fact that the Prophet himself was initially in agreement with him. The Prophet had to accept the other view when he realized that it was held by the broad majority of the Muslims. The Prophet did not see anything wrong with discarding his own view in favour of that of the majority.
Source: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 21: The Lessons of Uhud

output.scene_title: The Prophet Listens to the Majority
output.source_reference: Adil Salahi, Muhammad: Man and Prophet, Ch. 21: The Lessons of Uhud

characters.name: Prophet Muhammad (PBUH)
characters.appearance: Late 50s, medium build, dignified upright posture. Wearing a simple white thobe with a green cloak draped over shoulders, white turban with tail between shoulder blades. Simple leather sandals. Always shown from behind, face never visible. Distinguished by green cloak and subtle warm light.

characters.name: Umar ibn Al-Khattab
characters.appearance: Late 40s, medium olive skin, athletic build, slightly taller than average. Thick beard with slight reddish henna tint, neatly kept. Wearing off-white cotton thobe, plain brown woolen cloak, white cloth wrapped as loose head covering. Worn leather sandals. Strong determined eyes, upright posture.

output.moral_lesson: It's okay to change your mind when you realize most people have a better idea.
output.narrative_text: The Prophet Muhammad had a different idea about how to deal with the enemy, but some of his friends thought it was a better idea to fight them right away. When he thought about it, he decided to listen to the majority and change his plan. He learned that sometimes it's good to put aside your own opinion and do what most people think is best.

compliance.notes: Prophet shown from behind, standing in a group of men. Umar shown with full face, standing confidently. No graphic details about the battle. Appropriate for children.

output.image_prompt: Watercolor illustration of a 7th century mud-brick courtyard in Madinah. A group of men in simple white thobes, some with brown cloaks, gathered in a circle. One man in a white thobe and green cloak, white turban, stands from behind, looking at the men. Another man, slightly taller, in an off-white thobe and brown cloak, with a thick beard, stands confidently looking at the Prophet. Simple wooden pillars, clay water jug in a corner. Medium shot, eye level. Earthy palette. No modern objects.
